data.reasoningText:
Let me analyze what needs to change for xUnit.net v3 migration:

1. **`xunit` v2.9.3** → `xunit.v3` (the v3 package)
2. **`xunit.runner.visualstudio` v2.8.2** → `xunit.runner.visualstudio` v3.x (or it might be bundled)
3. **`Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`** → may still be needed or may change
4. **`Xunit.Combinatorial`** → need to check v3 compatibility. In xUnit v3, `CombinatorialData` may be built-in or need a different package.


5. **`Xunit.StaFact`** → also need to verify v3 support

Looking at the migration path, the main package swap is `xunit` to `xunit.v3`, with the test runner staying largely the same. Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk remains necessary, and combinatorial testing appears to be integrated directly into v3 rather than requiring a separate package. I should check whether `StaFact` has a v3-compatible version or if that functionality has been absorbed into the core framework. I need to check NuGet for the current versions of these packages to get the exact naming and compatibility details for xUnit v3 support, particularly for the Combinatorial and StaFact extensions.

## xUnit.net v3

xUnit.net is a developer testing framework, built to support Test Driven Development, with a design goal of extreme simplicity and alignment with framework features. Supports .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later and .NET 8 or later.

Installing this package installs xunit.v3.mtp-v1.

## About xUnit.net

xUnit.net is a free, open source, community-focused unit testing tool for C#, F#, and Visual Basic. xUnit.net v3 supports .NET 8.0 or later, and .NET Framework 4.7.2 or later.

xUnit.net works with the [.NET SDK](https://dotnet.microsoft.com/download) command line tools, [Visual Studio](https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/), [Visual Studio Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/), [JetBrains Rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/), [NCrunch](https://www.ncrunch.net/), and any development environment compatible with [Microsoft Testing Platform](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/testing/microsoft-testing-platform-intro) or [VSTest](https://github.com/microsoft/vstest).

xUnit.net is part of the [.NET Foundation](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/) and operates under their [code of conduct](https://www.dotnetfoundation.org/code-of-conduct). Migration complete. Here's a summary of the changes:

**`TestProject.csproj`:**
- `xunit` 2.9.3 → `xunit.v3` 3.2.2
- Removed `xunit.runner.visualstudio` and `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` (xUnit v3 bundles its own runner via MTP)
- `Xunit.Combinatorial` 1.6.24 → 2.0.24 (v3-compatible)
- `Xunit.StaFact` 1.1.11 → 3.0.13 (v3-compatible)
- Added `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` (required by xUnit v3)

**`global.json`:**
- Test runner changed from `VSTest` to `Microsoft.Testing.Platform`

**`CombinatorialTests.cs`:** No changes needed — the APIs (`[Theory]`, `[CombinatorialData]`, `[StaFact]`, `Assert`) remain compatible.
